selected_goal.title: Migrate the snake game from terminal/curses to a standalone pygame GUI window
selected_goal.rationale: The user's original goal is to build a snake game. The default product fit for a Python snake game is a standalone GUI window, not a terminal/curses interface. Pygame provides the expected visual experience with independent window, proper event handling, and standard game loop.
acceptance_criteria: Running '.venv/bin/python main.py' opens a pygame window instead of launching in the terminal
The snake moves continuously in the window and changes direction with arrow keys
A red square appears as food and the snake grows when eating it
The current score is displayed on the window (e.g., as a title or overlay)
When the snake hits a wall or itself, a 'Game Over' screen appears showing the final score and offering restart (R key) and quit (Q key) options
The game runs without any terminal dependencies (no curses module import)

selected_goal.title: Migrate snake game to pygame with game-over screen and score display
selected_goal.rationale: Product fit recommends pygame for standalone GUI games; the current tkinter implementation lacks a proper game-over screen. This migration ensures a better user experience and aligns with default expectations.
acceptance_criteria: main.py uses pygame (no tkinter) and runs without errors after 'pip install pygame'.
Game window opens with snake (green body, yellow head) and red food on a black background.
Arrow keys and WASD control direction; opposing direction is ignored.
Eating food increments score and spawns new food at a random location.
Collision with wall or self displays a game-over screen with final score and instructions: R to restart, Q to quit.
README.md updated to list pygame as dependency and reflect the run command.
No syntax or import errors when executing the script (assuming pygame is installed).

designed_tasks.description: Rewrite main.py to use pygame, implementing complete snake game logic with game-over screen and score display, replacing the current tkinter implementation.
acceptance_criteria: main.py imports and uses pygame only (no tkinter imports) and runs without errors after 'pip install pygame'.
Game window opens with a black background, snake rendered with green body and yellow head, and red food square.
Arrow keys and WASD control direction; pressing the opposite direction of current movement is ignored.
Eating food increments score by 1 and spawns new food at a random location within the grid.
Collision with wall or snake body triggers a game-over screen displaying the final score and instructions: 'R to Restart, Q to Quit'.
Pressing R during game-over restarts the game; pressing Q quits the application.
No syntax or import errors when executing the script (assuming pygame is installed).
risk_notes: Requires pygame to be installed in the virtual environment.
Ensure no tkinter or curses modules are imported.
Avoid infinite loops; use pygame's event loop properly.
Handle window close event gracefully.

failed_actions: Rewrite main.py to use pygame, implementing complete snake game logic with game-over screen and score display, replacing the current tkinter implementation.

selected_goal.title: Migrate snake game to pygame with game-over screen and score display
selected_goal.rationale: Product fit recommends pygame for standalone GUI games; the current tkinter implementation lacks a proper game-over screen. This migration ensures a better user experience and aligns with default expectations.
acceptance_criteria: main.py uses pygame (no tkinter) and runs without errors after 'pip install pygame'.
Game window opens with snake (green body, yellow head) and red food on a black background.
Arrow keys and WASD control direction; opposing direction is ignored.
Eating food increments score and spawns new food at a random location.
Collision with wall or self displays a game-over screen with final score and instructions: R to restart, Q to quit.
README.md updated to list pygame as dependency and reflect the run command.
No syntax or import errors when executing the script (assuming pygame is installed).

designed_tasks.description: Rewrite main.py to use pygame, implementing complete snake game logic with game-over screen and score display, replacing the current tkinter implementation.
acceptance_criteria: main.py imports and uses pygame only (no tkinter imports) and runs without errors after 'pip install pygame'.
Game window opens with a black background, snake rendered with green body and yellow head, and red food square.
Arrow keys and WASD control direction; pressing the opposite direction of current movement is ignored.
Eating food increments score by 1 and spawns new food at a random location within the grid.
Collision with wall or snake body triggers a game-over screen displaying the final score and instructions: 'R to Restart, Q to Quit'.
Pressing R during game-over restarts the game; pressing Q quits the application.
No syntax or import errors when executing the script (assuming pygame is installed).
risk_notes: Requires pygame to be installed in the virtual environment.
Ensure no tkinter or curses modules are imported.
Avoid infinite loops; use pygame's event loop properly.
Handle window close event gracefully.
